locator.paragraph_or_text_span: NICE guideline Reference number: NG245 Published: 27 November 2024 Download guidance (PDF)

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: 1.1 Initial clinical assessment 1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma 1.3 Diagnosing asthma in children under 5 1.4 Diagnosing occupational asthma 1.5 Monitoring asthma control 1.6 Principles of pharmacological treatment 1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over 1.8 Pharmacological management

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: People have the right to be involved in discussions and make informed decisions about their care, as described in NICE's information on making decisions about your care . Information about decision making is also available from Realistic medicine . Making decisions using NICE guidelines and Using SIGN guidelines explains how we use words to show the strength (or certainty) of our recommendations,

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Obtain a structured clinical history in people with suspected asthma. Specifically, check for: reported wheeze, noisy breathing, cough, breathlessness or chest tightness, and any variation (for example, worse during the night or early morning, or seasonal) in these symptoms any triggers that make symptoms worse a personal or family history of asthma or allergic rhinitis symptoms to suggest alterna

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not confirm a diagnosis of asthma without a suggestive clinical history and a supporting objective test. Code as suspected asthma until the diagnosis is confirmed. [NICE 2017, amended BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If the diagnosis of asthma is confirmed, record the basis for this in the person's medical records, alongside the coded diagnostic entry. [NICE 2017, amended BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Examine people with suspected asthma to identify expiratory polyphonic wheeze and signs of other causes of respiratory symptoms but be aware that even if examination results are normal, the person may still have asthma. [NICE 2017]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
Initial treatment and objective tests for acute symptoms at presentation
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Treat people immediately if they are acutely unwell or highly symptomatic at presentation, and perform objective tests that may help support a diagnosis of asthma (for example, eosinophil count , fractional exhaled nitric oxide [FeNO], spirometry or peak expiratory flow [PEF] before and after bronchodilator) if the equipment is available. [NICE 2017, amended BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
Initial treatment and objective tests for acute symptoms at presentation
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If objective tests for asthma cannot be done immediately for people who are acutely unwell or highly symptomatic at presentation, carry them out when acute symptoms have been controlled, and advise people to contact their healthcare professional immediately if they become unwell while waiting to have objective tests. [NICE 2017, amended BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
Initial treatment and objective tests for acute symptoms at presentation
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Be aware that the results of spirometry and FeNO tests may be affected in people who have been treated with inhaled corticosteroids (the test results are more likely to be normal). [NICE 2017]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: See also algorithm A for a summary of objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults and young people (aged over 16 years) with a history suggesting asthma .

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Measure the blood eosinophil count or fractional exhaled nitric oxide (FeNO) level in adults with a history suggestive of asthma. Diagnose asthma if the eosinophil count is above the laboratory reference range or the FeNO level is 50 ppb or more. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If asthma is not confirmed by eosinophil count or FeNO level, measure bronchodilator reversibility (BDR) with spirometry. Diagnose asthma if the FEV 1 increase is 12% or more and 200 ml or more from the pre-bronchodilator measurement (or if the FEV 1 increase is 10% or more of the predicted normal FEV 1 ). [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If spirometry is not available or it is delayed, measure peak expiratory flow (PEF) twice daily for 2 weeks. Diagnose asthma if PEF variability (expressed as amplitude percentage mean) is 20% or more. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If asthma is not confirmed by eosinophil count, FeNO, BDR or PEF variability but still suspected on clinical grounds, refer for consideration of a bronchial challenge test . Diagnose asthma if bronchial hyper-responsiveness is present. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: See also algorithm B for a summary of objective tests for diagnosing asthma in children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggesting asthma .

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Measure the FeNO level in children with a history suggestive of asthma. Diagnose asthma if the FeNO level is 35 ppb or more. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If the FeNO level is not raised, or if FeNO testing is not available, measure BDR with spirometry. Diagnose asthma if the FEV 1 increase is 12% or more from baseline (or if the FEV 1 increase is 10% or more of the predicted normal FEV 1 ). [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If spirometry is not available or it is delayed, measure PEF twice daily for 2 weeks. Diagnose asthma if PEF variability (expressed as amplitude percentage mean) is 20% or more. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If asthma is not confirmed by FeNO, BDR or PEF variability but still suspected on clinical grounds, either perform skin prick testing to house dust mite or measure total IgE level and blood eosinophil count. Exclude asthma if there is no evidence of sensitisation to house dust mite on skin prick testing or if the total serum IgE is not raised. Diagnose asthma if there is evidence of sensitisation

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.2 Objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 with a history suggestive of asthma
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If there is still doubt about the diagnosis, refer to a paediatric specialist for a second opinion, including consideration of a bronchial challenge test. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024] For a short explanation of why the committee made these recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children a

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Diagnosis is hard in this age group because it is difficult to do the tests and there are no good reference standards.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For children under 5 with suspected asthma, treat with inhaled corticosteroids in line with the recommendations on medicines for initial management in children under 5 , and review the child on a regular basis. If they still have symptoms when they reach 5 years, attempt objective tests (see the section on objective tests for diagnosing asthma in adults, young people and children aged 5 to 16 ). [

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If a child is unable to perform objective tests when they are aged 5: try doing the tests again every 6 to 12 months until satisfactory results are obtained refer for specialist assessment if the child's asthma is not responding to treatment. [NICE 2017, BTS/SIGN 2019, amended BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Refer to a specialist respiratory paediatrician any preschool child with an admission to hospital, or 2 or more admissions to an emergency department, with wheeze in a 12-month period. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: See the BTS clinical statement on occupational asthma .

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: In people with adult-onset asthma, poorly controlled established asthma, or reappearance of childhood asthma, check for a possible occupational component by asking: Are symptoms the same, better or worse on days away from work? Are symptoms the same, better or worse when on holiday (time away from work, longer than usual breaks, at weekends or between shifts)? Make sure all answers are recorded fo

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Refer people with suspected occupational asthma to an occupational asthma specialist. [NICE 2017]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Monitor asthma control at every review. In addition to asking about symptoms, check: time off work or school due to asthma amount of reliever inhaler used, including a check of the prescription record number of courses of oral corticosteroids any admissions to hospital or attendance at an emergency department due to asthma. If control is suboptimal, see recommendation 1.6.1 in the section on princ

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider using a validated symptom questionnaire (for example, the Asthma Control Questionnaire, the Asthma Control Test or the Childhood Asthma Control Test) at any asthma review. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use regular peak expiratory flow (PEF) monitoring to assess asthma control unless there are person-specific reasons for doing so (for example, when PEF measurement is part of the personalised asthma action plan). [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider fractional exhaled nitric oxide (FeNO) monitoring for adults with asthma: at their regular review, and before and after changing their asthma therapy. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024] For a short explanation of why the committee made these recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on monitoring asthma control . Full details of the evidence and the commit

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Licensed indications for asthma inhalers vary between different medicines, different doses and different devices. Not all asthma inhalers are licensed for use in line with the recommendations in this guideline. See NICE's information on prescribing medicines or SIGN's information on prescribing licensed medicines out with their marketing authorisation and refer to the summary of product characteri

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Take into account and try to address the possible reasons for uncontrolled asthma before starting or adjusting medicines for asthma in adults, young people and children. These may include: alternative diagnoses or comorbidities suboptimal adherence (see the recommendation on adherence ) suboptimal inhaler technique smoking (active or passive), including vaping using e-cigarettes occupational expos

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If possible, check the fractional exhaled nitric oxide (FeNO) level when asthma is uncontrolled. If it is raised this may indicate poor adherence to treatment or the need for an increased dose of inhaled corticosteroid (ICS). [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not prescribe short-acting beta 2 agonists to people of any age with asthma without a concomitant prescription of an ICS. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: After starting or adjusting medicines for asthma, review the response to treatment in 8 to 12 weeks (see the recommendations on monitoring asthma control ). [NICE 2017, amended BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024] For a short explanation of why the committee made these 2024 recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on principles of pharmacological treatment . Full det

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Base the choice of inhaler(s) for asthma on: an assessment of correct technique the preference of the person receiving the treatment the lowest environmental impact among suitable devices the presence of an integral dose counter. A spacer should usually be prescribed for use with a metered dose inhaler, particularly in children. See the patient decision aid on asthma inhalers and climate change .

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Give people with asthma information on their inhaler treatments. This should include the medicines they contain, how they work, when they should be taken and the correct technique to use for each device. [BTS/SIGN 2019, amended BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Observe the person using their inhaler device (and spacer if used) to check they can use it properly: at every asthma review, either routine or unscheduled at every asthma-related consultation when there is deterioration in asthma control when the inhaler device is changed when the person asks for it to be checked or changed. If the person is assessed as being unable to use a device properly, find

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If possible, prescribe the same type of device to deliver preventer and reliever treatments where more than one inhaler is needed. Consider providing an additional metered dose short-acting beta 2 agonist (SABA) inhaler plus spacer for emergency use for children under 12 years who may be unable to activate a dry powder inhaler during an acute asthma attack. [BTS/SIGN 2019, amended BTS/NICE/SIGN 20

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Encourage people to take their used or expired inhalers to their pharmacy for disposal. [BTS/SIGN 2019]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Digital inhalers are not recommended for routine use in people with asthma. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024] For a short explanation of why the committee made this 2024 recommendation and how it might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on digital inhalers . Full details of the evidence and the committee's discussion are in evidence review R: smart inhalers .

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: See also algorithm C for a summary of the pharmacological management of asthma in people aged 12 years and over .

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Initial management of newly diagnosed asthma in people aged 12 and over
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer a low-dose inhaled corticosteroid (ICS)/formoterol combination inhaler to be taken as needed for symptom relief (as-needed AIR therapy ) to people aged 12 and over with newly diagnosed asthma. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024] In November 2025, only certain budesonide/formoterol inhalers were licensed for as-needed AIR therapy in mild asthma. The use of any other ICS/formoterol inhalers would therefore b

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Initial management of newly diagnosed asthma in people aged 12 and over
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If the person needing asthma treatment presents highly symptomatic (for example, regular nocturnal waking) or with a severe exacerbation, start treatment with low-dose MART ( maintenance and reliever therapy ) in addition to treating the acute symptoms as indicated (that is, a course of oral corticosteroids may be needed). Consider stepping down to as-needed AIR therapy using a low-dose ICS/formot

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Medicine combination and sequencing in people aged 12 and over
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For guidance on dose ranges of inhaled corticosteroids see inhaled corticosteroid doses for the BTS, NICE and SIGN asthma guideline .

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Medicine combination and sequencing in people aged 12 and over
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer low-dose MART to people aged 12 and over with asthma that is not controlled on a low-dose ICS/formoterol combination inhaler used only as needed. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Medicine combination and sequencing in people aged 12 and over
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer moderate-dose MART to people aged 12 and over with asthma that is not controlled on low-dose MART. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Medicine combination and sequencing in people aged 12 and over
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For people aged 12 and over with asthma that is not controlled on moderate-dose MART despite good adherence: Check the fractional exhaled nitric oxide (FeNO) level if available, and the blood eosinophil count . If either of these is raised, refer to a specialist in asthma care . If neither FeNO or eosinophil count is raised, consider a trial of either a leukotriene receptor antagonist (LTRA) or a

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Medicine combination and sequencing in people aged 12 and over
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Refer people to a specialist in asthma care when asthma is not controlled despite treatment with moderate-dose MART, and trials of an LTRA and a LAMA. (See the Accelerated Access Collaborative consensus pathway on the management of uncontrolled asthma in adults .) [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024] For a short explanation of why the committee made these recommendations and how they might affect practice, see th

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Transferring people aged 12 and over from other treatment pathways
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: These recommendations are for people with uncontrolled asthma who are on the treatment pathway recommended by previous NICE and BTS/SIGN guidelines.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Transferring people aged 12 and over from other treatment pathways
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Change treatment for people with confirmed asthma who are currently using a short-acting beta 2 agonist (SABA) only to a low-dose ICS/formoterol combination inhaler used as needed (as-needed AIR therapy). [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Transferring people aged 12 and over from other treatment pathways
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider changing treatment to low-dose MART for people with asthma that is not controlled on: regular low-dose ICS plus SABA as needed regular low-dose ICS/LABA ( long-acting beta 2 agonist ) combination inhaler plus SABA as needed regular low-dose ICS and supplementary therapy (LTRA) plus SABA as needed. regular low-dose ICS/LABA combination inhaler and supplementary therapy (LTRA) plus SABA as

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Transferring people aged 12 and over from other treatment pathways
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider changing treatment to moderate-dose MART for people with asthma that is not controlled on: regular moderate-dose ICS plus SABA as needed regular moderate-dose ICS/LABA combination inhaler plus SABA as needed regular moderate-dose ICS and supplementary therapy (LTRA or LAMA, or both) plus SABA as needed regular moderate-dose ICS/LABA combination inhaler and supplementary therapy (LTRA or L

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Transferring people aged 12 and over from other treatment pathways
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: When changing from low- or moderate-dose ICS (or ICS/LABA combination inhaler) plus supplementary therapy to MART, consider whether to stop or continue the supplementary therapy based on the degree of benefit achieved when first introduced. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.7 Pharmacological management in people aged 12 and over
Transferring people aged 12 and over from other treatment pathways
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Refer people with asthma that is not controlled on treatment containing a high dose of ICS to a specialist in asthma care. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024] For a short explanation of why the committee made these recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on transferring people aged 12 and over from other treatment pathways . Full details of the evidence and the co

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.8 Pharmacological management in children aged 5 to 11
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For guidance on doses on inhaled corticosteroids see inhaled corticosteroid doses for the BTS, NICE and SIGN asthma guideline . See also algorithm D for a summary of the pharmacological management of asthma in children aged 5 to 11 years .

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.8 Pharmacological management in children aged 5 to 11
Initial management in children aged 5 to 11
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer a twice-daily paediatric low-dose inhaled corticosteroid (ICS), with a short-acting beta 2 agonist (SABA) as needed, as initial treatment for children aged 5 to 11 years with newly diagnosed asthma. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024] For a short explanation of why the committee made this recommendation and how it might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on medicines for initial manageme

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.8 Pharmacological management in children aged 5 to 11
Medicine combination and sequencing in children aged 5 to 11
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider paediatric low-dose MART ( maintenance and reliever therapy ) for children with asthma that is not controlled on paediatric low-dose ICS plus SABA as needed, as long as they are assessed to have the ability to manage a MART regimen. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024] In November 2025, only 1 budesonide/formoterol dry powder inhaler (100 micrograms/6 micrograms per inhalation) was licensed for MART in c

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.8 Pharmacological management in children aged 5 to 11
Medicine combination and sequencing in children aged 5 to 11
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider increasing to paediatric moderate-dose MART if asthma is not controlled on paediatric low-dose MART. [BTS/NICE/SIGN 2024]

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
1.8 Pharmacological management in children aged 5 to 11

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: This section defines terms that have been used in a particular way for this guideline. For other definitions see the NICE glossary and the Think Local, Act Personal Care and Support Jargon Buster .

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Anti-inflammatory reliever (AIR) therapy is treatment with a reliever inhaler that contains a combination of an inhaled corticosteroid and formoterol. When this is used in response to symptoms without regular maintenance therapy it is called as-needed AIR therapy. In November 2025 the only products licensed for as-needed AIR therapy contained budesonide/formoterol.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Complete control of asthma is defined as no daytime symptoms, no night-time awakening due to asthma, no asthma attacks, no need for rescue medication, no limitations on activity including exercise, normal lung function (in practical terms forced expiratory volume in 1 second [ FEV 1 ] and/or peak expiratory flow [PEF] more than 80% predicted or best), and minimal side effects from treatment.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Atopic disorders are allergic conditions including allergic rhinitis (hay fever), atopic dermatitis (eczema), allergic asthma and other specific and non-specific allergic conditions such as food allergies.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A test to measure airway responsiveness (bronchial responsiveness). It is performed by giving small increments of a bronchoconstrictor (most commonly methacholine) and measuring the FEV 1 after each dose until it falls by a predetermined amount (usually 20% from baseline).

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A measure of how easily bronchospasm can be induced in the airways. It is measured using a bronchial challenge test.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A measure of the ability to reverse obstruction in the airways using medicines that widen the airways (bronchodilators).

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The number of eosinophils (a type of white blood cell) measured in a blood sample. Their levels are raised in asthma and other allergic diseases, and less commonly with malignant diseases, parasite infections, reactions to some medicines, and a small number of rare diseases.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A test that measures the amount of nitric oxide (NO) present on exhalation, usually expressed in parts per billion.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The amount of air that can be forcibly exhaled from the lungs in one second (forced expiratory volume in one second).

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A type of oral medicine that blocks cysteinyl leukotrienes, used in the treatment of asthma and seasonal allergies. Also known as leukotriene modifiers.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A long-acting medicine that acts on beta-receptors in the airway to relax airway smooth muscle and relieve symptoms of asthma.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A long-acting medicine that acts on muscarinic receptors in the airway to relax airway smooth muscle and relieve symptoms of asthma.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A form of combined ICS plus formoterol treatment in which a single inhaler containing ICS and formoterol is used for daily maintenance therapy and the relief of symptoms as needed. The terms low-dose MART and moderate-dose MART refer to the dosage of the maintenance component of MART. People using MART do not normally need a SABA.

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: PEF is a measure of the maximum speed of expiration, generally expressed in litres per minute. PEF variability is a measure of the extent to which this varies over time and can be expressed numerically as amplitude percentage mean. locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A test that measures the allergic response of an individual to certain specific allergens when a very small amount of the specific allergen is introduced into the skin (usually the inner forearm).

heading_path: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management (BTS, NICE, SIGN)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A healthcare professional with higher training in respiratory medicine and proficiency in the management of asthma. In the context of this guideline, this requires both the relevant expertise and access to the resources that enable delivery of the diagnostic and management pathways described in the recommendations.

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The diagnosis of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) depends on thinking of it as a cause of breathlessness or cough. The diagnosis is suspected on the basis of symptoms and signs and is supported by spirometry.

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Perform spirometry in people who are over 35, current or ex‑smokers, and have a chronic cough. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider spirometry in people with chronic bronchitis. A significant proportion of these people will go on to develop airflow limitation. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: When clinically indicated, refer people for specialist advice. Referral may be appropriate at all stages of the disease and not solely in the most severely disabled people (see table 5). [2004] Table 5 Reasons for referral Reason Purpose There is diagnostic uncertainty Confirm diagnosis and optimise therapy Suspected severe COPD Confirm diagnosis and optimise therapy The person with COPD requests

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: People who are referred do not always have to be seen by a respiratory physician. In some cases they may be seen by members of the COPD team who have appropriate training and expertise. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: NICE has also produced a visual summary covering non-pharmacological management and use of inhaled therapies .

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For guidance on the management of multimorbidity, see the NICE guideline on multimorbidity . [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Document an up-to-date smoking history, including pack years smoked (number of cigarettes smoked per day, divided by 20, multiplied by the number of years smoked) for everyone with COPD. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: At every opportunity, advise and encourage every person with COPD who is still smoking (regardless of their age) to stop, and offer them help to do so. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer stop-smoking interventions as appropriate to people who want to stop smoking, combined with an appropriate support programme to optimise smoking quit rates for people with COPD. [2010]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For more guidance on helping people to quit smoking, see the NICE guideline on tobacco: preventing uptake, promoting quitting and treating dependence . [2010]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Short-acting beta2 agonists (SABA) and short-acting muscarinic antagonists (SAMA)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Use short-acting bronchodilators, as necessary, as the initial empirical treatment to relieve breathlessness and exercise limitation. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use oral corticosteroid reversibility tests to identify which people should be prescribed inhaled corticosteroids, because they do not predict response to inhaled corticosteroid therapy. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Be aware of, and be prepared to discuss with the person, the risk of side effects (including pneumonia) in people who take inhaled corticosteroids for COPD. Follow the MHRA safety advice on the risk of psychological and behavioural side effects associated with inhaled corticosteroids. [2010, amended 2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Inhaled combination therapy refers to combinations of long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMA), long-acting beta2 agonists (LABA), and inhaled corticosteroids (ICS).

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not assess the effectiveness of bronchodilator therapy using lung function alone. Include a variety of other measures such as improvement in symptoms, activities of daily living, exercise capacity, and rapidity of symptom relief. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer LAMA+LABA to people who: have spirometrically confirmed COPD and do not have asthmatic features/features suggesting steroid responsiveness and remain breathless or have exacerbations despite: having used or been offered treatment for tobacco dependence if they smoke and optimised non-pharmacological management and relevant vaccinations and using a short-acting bronchodilator. [2018] Follow t

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider LABA+ICS for people who: have spirometrically confirmed COPD and have asthmatic features/features suggesting steroid responsiveness and remain breathless or have exacerbations despite: having used or been offered treatment for tobacco dependence if they smoke and optimised non-pharmacological management and relevant vaccinations and using a short-acting bronchodilator. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For people who are using long-acting bronchodilators outside of the recommendations on offering LAMA and LABA and considering LABA+ICS and whose symptoms are under control, explain to them that they can continue with their current treatment until both they and their NHS healthcare professional agree it is appropriate to change. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before starting LAMA+LABA+ICS, conduct a clinical review to ensure that: the person's non-pharmacological COPD management is optimised and they have used or been offered treatment for tobacco dependence if they smoke acute episodes of worsening symptoms are caused by COPD exacerbations and not by another physical or mental health condition the person's day-to-day symptoms that are adversely impact

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For people with COPD who are taking LABA+ICS, offer LAMA+LABA+ICS if: their day-to-day symptoms continue to adversely impact their quality of life or they have a severe exacerbation (requiring hospitalisation) or they have 2 moderate exacerbations within a year. [2019]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For people with COPD who are taking LAMA+LABA, consider LAMA+LABA+ICS if: they have a severe exacerbation (requiring hospitalisation) or they have 2 moderate exacerbations within a year. [2019]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For people with COPD who are taking LAMA+LABA and whose day-to-day symptoms adversely impact their quality of life: consider a trial of LAMA+LABA+ICS, lasting for 3 months only after 3 months, conduct a clinical review to establish whether or not LAMA+LABA+ICS has improved their symptoms: if symptoms have not improved, stop LAMA+LABA+ICS and switch back to LAMA+LABA if symptoms have improved, cont

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Document the reason for continuing ICS use in clinical records and review at least annually. [2019]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Base the choice of drugs and inhalers on: how much they improve symptoms the person's preferences and ability to use the inhalers the drugs' potential to reduce exacerbations their side effects their cost. Minimise the number of inhalers and the number of different types of inhaler used by each person as far as possible. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: When prescribing long-acting drugs, ensure people receive inhalers they have been trained to use (for example, by specifying the brand and inhaler in prescriptions). [2018] For a short explanation of why the committee made the 2018 and 2019 recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on inhaled combination therapy. Full details of the evidence and the c

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Delivery systems used to treat stable COPD
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Most people with COPD – whatever their age – can develop adequate inhaler technique if they are given training. However, people with significant cognitive impairment may be unable to use any form of inhaler device. In most people with COPD, however, a pragmatic approach guided by individual patient assessment is needed when choosing a device.

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: In most cases bronchodilator therapy is best administered using a hand-held inhaler (including a spacer if appropriate). [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Provide an alternative inhaler if a person cannot use a particular one correctly or it is not suitable for them. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Only prescribe inhalers after people have been trained to use them and can demonstrate satisfactory technique. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: People with COPD should have their ability to use an inhaler regularly assessed and corrected if necessary by a healthcare professional competent to do so. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Provide a spacer that is compatible with the person's metered-dose inhaler. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people to use a spacer with a metered-dose inhaler in the following way: administer the drug by single actuations of the metered-dose inhaler into the spacer, inhaling after each actuation there should be minimal delay between inhaler actuation and inhalation normal tidal breathing can be used as it is as effective as single breaths repeat if a second dose is required. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people on spacer cleaning. Tell them: not to clean the spacer more than monthly, because more frequent cleaning affects their performance (because of a build-up of static) to hand wash using warm water and washing-up liquid, and allow the spacer to air dry. [2004, amended 2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Think about nebuliser therapy for people with distressing or disabling breathlessness despite maximal therapy using inhalers. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not prescribe nebulised therapy without an assessment of the person's and/or carer's ability to use it. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not continue nebulised therapy without assessing and confirming that 1 or more of the following occurs: a reduction in symptoms an increase in the ability to undertake activities of daily living an increase in exercise capacity an improvement in lung function. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Use a nebuliser system that is known to be efficient. [2004] Follow the MHRA safety advice on non-CE-marked nebulisers for COPD .

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer people a choice between a facemask and a mouthpiece to administer their nebulised therapy, unless the drug specifically requires a mouthpiece (for example, anticholinergic drugs). [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If nebuliser therapy is prescribed, provide the person with equipment, servicing, and ongoing advice and support. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Long-term use of oral corticosteroid therapy in COPD is not normally recommended. Some people with advanced COPD may need long-term oral corticosteroids when these cannot be withdrawn following an exacerbation. In these cases, the dose of oral corticosteroids should be kept as low as possible. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Monitor people who are having long-term oral corticosteroid therapy for osteoporosis, and give them appropriate prophylaxis. Start prophylaxis without monitoring for people over 65. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: In this section of the guideline, the term theophylline refers to slow-release formulations of the drug.

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Theophylline should only be used after a trial of short-acting bronchodilators and long-acting bronchodilators, or for people who are unable to use inhaled therapy, as plasma levels and interactions need to be monitored. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Take particular caution when using theophylline in older people, because of differences in pharmacokinetics, the increased likelihood of comorbidities and the use of other medications. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Assess the effectiveness of theophylline by improvements in symptoms, activities of daily living, exercise capacity and lung function. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Reduce the dose of theophylline for people who are having an exacerbation if they are prescribed macrolide or fluoroquinolone antibiotics (or other drugs known to interact). [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider mucolytic drug therapy for people with a chronic cough productive of sputum. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Only continue mucolytic therapy if there is symptomatic improvement (for example, reduction in frequency of cough and sputum production). [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not routinely use mucolytic drugs to prevent exacerbations in people with stable COPD. [2010]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Treatment with alpha-tocopherol and beta-carotene supplements, alone or in combination, is not recommended. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Anti-tussive therapy should not be used in the management of stable COPD. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before starting prophylactic antibiotic therapy in a person with COPD, think about whether respiratory specialist input is needed. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider azithromycin (usually 250 mg 3 times a week) for people with COPD if they: do not smoke and have optimised non-pharmacological management and inhaled therapies, relevant vaccinations and (if appropriate) have been referred for pulmonary rehabilitation and continue to have 1 or more of the following, particularly if they have significant daily sputum production: frequent (typically 4 or mo

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before offering prophylactic antibiotics, ensure that the person has had: sputum culture and sensitivity (including tuberculosis culture), to identify other possible causes of persistent or recurrent infection that may need specific treatment (for example, antibiotic-resistant organisms, atypical mycobacteria or Pseudomonas aeruginosa ) training in airway clearance techniques to optimise sputum cl

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before starting azithromycin, ensure the person has had: an electrocardiogram (ECG) to rule out prolonged QT interval and baseline liver function tests. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: When prescribing azithromycin, advise people about the small risk of hearing loss and tinnitus, and tell them to contact a healthcare professional if this occurs. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Review prophylactic azithromycin after the first 3 months, and then at least every 6 months. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Only continue treatment if the continued benefits outweigh the risks. Be aware that there are no long-term studies on the use of prophylactic antibiotics in people with COPD. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For people who are taking prophylactic azithromycin and are still at risk of exacerbations, provide a non-macrolide antibiotic to keep at home as part of their exacerbation action plan (see the recommendation on offering antibiotics to keep at home in the section on self-management ). [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Be aware that it is not necessary to stop prophylactic azithromycin during an acute exacerbation of COPD. [2018] For a short explanation of why the committee made the 2018 recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on oral prophylactic antibiotic therapy . Full details of the evidence and the committee's discussion are in evidence review E: Predicting

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Roflumilast is recommended as an option in NICE technology appraisal guidance for treating COPD in adults with chronic bronchitis if the disease is severe, the person has had 2 or more exacerbations in the previous 12 months despite triple inhaled therapy, and treatment is started by a specialist. For full details, see the guidance on roflumilast (TA461, 2017) .

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Be aware that inappropriate oxygen therapy in people with COPD may cause respiratory depression. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Assess the need for oxygen therapy in people with: very severe airflow obstruction (FEV1 below 30% predicted) cyanosis (blue tint to skin) polycythaemia peripheral oedema (swelling) a raised jugular venous pressure oxygen saturations of 92% or less breathing air. Also consider assessment for people with severe airflow obstruction (FEV1 30–49% predicted). Be aware that some pulse oximeters can unde

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Assess people for long-term oxygen therapy by measuring arterial blood gases on 2 occasions at least 3 weeks apart in people who have a confident diagnosis of COPD, who are receiving optimum medical management and whose COPD is stable. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider long-term oxygen therapy for people with COPD who do not smoke and who: have a partial pressure of oxygen in arterial blood (PaO 2 ) below 7.3 kPa when stable or have a PaO 2 above 7.3 and below 8 kPa when stable, if they also have 1 or more of the following: secondary polycythaemia peripheral oedema pulmonary hypertension. See the MHRA alert on the risk of death and severe harm from fail

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Conduct and document a structured risk assessment for people being assessed for long-term oxygen therapy who meet the criteria in the recommendation on considering long-term oxygen therapy . As part of the risk assessment, cover the risks for both the person with COPD and the people who live with them, including: the risks of falls from tripping over the equipment the risks of burns and fires, and

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For people who smoke or live with people who smoke, but who meet the other criteria for long-term oxygen therapy, ensure the person who smokes is offered smoking cessation advice and treatment, and referral to specialist stop smoking services (see the NICE guidelines on tobacco: preventing uptake, promoting quitting and treating dependence and medicines optimisation ). [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not offer long-term oxygen therapy to people who continue to smoke despite being offered smoking cessation advice and treatment, and referral to specialist stop smoking services. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people who are having long-term oxygen therapy that they should breathe supplemental oxygen for a minimum of 15 hours per day. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not offer long-term oxygen therapy to treat isolated nocturnal hypoxaemia caused by COPD. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: To ensure everyone eligible for long-term oxygen therapy is identified, pulse oximetry should be available in all healthcare settings. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Oxygen concentrators should be used to provide the fixed supply at home for long-term oxygen therapy. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: People who are having long-term oxygen therapy should be reviewed at least once per year by healthcare professionals familiar with long-term oxygen therapy. This review should include pulse oximetry. [2004] For a short explanation of why the committee made the 2018 recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on long-term oxygen therapy . Full details of

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not offer ambulatory oxygen to manage breathlessness in people with COPD who have mild or no hypoxaemia at rest. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider ambulatory oxygen in people with COPD who have exercise desaturation and are shown to have an improvement in exercise capacity with oxygen, and have the motivation to use oxygen. [2004, amended 2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Prescribe ambulatory oxygen to people who are already on long-term oxygen therapy, who wish to continue oxygen therapy outside the home, and who are prepared to use it. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Only prescribe ambulatory oxygen therapy after an appropriate assessment has been performed by a specialist. The purpose of the assessment is to assess the extent of desaturation, the improvement in exercise capacity with supplemental oxygen, and the oxygen flow rate needed to correct desaturation. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Small light-weight cylinders, oxygen-conserving devices and portable liquid oxygen systems should be available for people with COPD. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: When choosing which equipment to prescribe, take account of the hours of ambulatory oxygen use and oxygen flow rate needed. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not offer short-burst oxygen therapy to manage breathlessness in people with COPD who have mild or no hypoxaemia at rest. [2018] For a short explanation of why the committee made the 2018 recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on ambulatory and oxygen short-burst oxygen therapy . Full details of the evidence and the committee's discussion are in

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Refer people who are adequately treated but have chronic hypercapnic respiratory failure and have needed assisted ventilation (whether invasive or non-invasive) during an exacerbation, or who are hypercapnic or acidotic on long-term oxygen therapy, to a specialist centre for consideration of long-term non-invasive ventilation. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Managing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: In this guideline 'cor pulmonale' is defined as a clinical condition that is identified and managed on the basis of clinical features. It includes people who have right heart failure secondary to lung disease and people whose primary pathology is salt and water retention, leading to the development of peripheral oedema (swelling).

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Managing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale
Diagnosing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Suspect a diagnosis of cor pulmonale for people with: peripheral oedema (swelling) a raised venous pressure a systolic parasternal heave a loud pulmonary second heart sound. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Managing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale
Diagnosing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: It is recommended that the diagnosis of cor pulmonale is made clinically and that this process should involve excluding other causes of peripheral oedema (swelling). [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Managing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not offer the following treatments solely to manage pulmonary hypertension caused by COPD, except as part of a randomised controlled trial: bosentan losartan nifedipine nitric oxide pentoxifylline phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitors statins. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Managing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Ensure that people with cor pulmonale caused by COPD are offered optimal COPD treatment, including advice and interventions to help them stop smoking. For people who need treatment for hypoxia, see the section on long-term oxygen therapy . [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Managing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Oedema associated with cor pulmonale can usually be controlled symptomatically with diuretic therapy. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Managing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use the following to treat cor pulmonale caused by COPD: alpha-blockers angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors calcium channel blockers digoxin (unless there is atrial fibrillation). [2018] For a short explanation of why the committee made the 2018 recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on managing pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Pulmonary rehabilitation is defined as a multidisciplinary programme of care for people with chronic respiratory impairment. It is individually tailored and designed to optimise each person's physical and social performance and autonomy.

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Make pulmonary rehabilitation available to all appropriate people with COPD (see the recommendation on offering pulmonary rehabilitation ), including people who have had a recent hospitalisation for an acute exacerbation. [2010]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer pulmonary rehabilitation to all people who view themselves as functionally disabled by COPD (usually Medical Research Council [MRC] grade 3 and above). Pulmonary rehabilitation is not suitable for people who are unable to walk, who have unstable angina or who have had a recent myocardial infarction. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For pulmonary rehabilitation programmes to be effective, and to improve adherence, they should be held at times that suit people, in buildings that are easy to get to and that have good access for people with disabilities. Places should be available within a reasonable time of referral. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Pulmonary rehabilitation programmes should include multicomponent, multidisciplinary interventions that are tailored to the individual person's needs. The rehabilitation process should incorporate a programme of physical training, disease education, and nutritional, psychological and behavioural intervention. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people of the benefits of pulmonary rehabilitation and the commitment needed to gain these. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer pneumococcal vaccination and an annual flu vaccination to all people with COPD, as recommended by the Chief Medical Officer. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Oseltamivir and zanamivir are recommended as options in NICE technology appraisal guidance for preventing and treating flu in some people in at-risk groups. For full details, see the guidance on oseltamivir and zanamivir for preventing flu (TA158, 2008) and treating flu (TA168, 2009) .

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Amantadine is not recommended in NICE technology appraisal guidance for preventing or treating flu. For full details, see the guidance on amantadine for preventing flu (TA158, 2008) and treating flu (TA168, 2009) .

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Lung surgery and lung volume reduction procedures
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer a respiratory review to assess whether a lung volume reduction procedure is a possibility for people with COPD when they complete pulmonary rehabilitation and at other subsequent reviews, if all of the following apply: they have severe COPD, with FEV1 less than 50% and breathlessness that affects their quality of life despite optimal medical treatment (see recommendations 1.2.11 to 1.2.17 in

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Lung surgery and lung volume reduction procedures
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: At the respiratory review, refer the person with COPD to a lung volume reduction multidisciplinary team to assess whether lung volume reduction surgery or endobronchial valves are suitable if they have: hyperinflation, assessed by lung function testing with body plethysmography and emphysema on unenhanced CT chest scan and optimised treatment for other comorbidities. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Lung surgery and lung volume reduction procedures
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Only offer endobronchial coils as part of a clinical trial and after assessment by a lung volume reduction multidisciplinary team. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Lung surgery and lung volume reduction procedures
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For more guidance on lung volume reduction procedures, see the NICE interventional procedures guidance on lung volume reduction surgery , endobronchial valves and endobronchial coils . [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Lung surgery and lung volume reduction procedures
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Refer people with COPD for an assessment for bullectomy if they are breathless and a CT scan shows a bulla occupying at least one third of the hemithorax. [2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
Lung surgery and lung volume reduction procedures

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: An exacerbation is a sustained worsening of the patient's symptoms from their usual stable state which is beyond normal day-to-day variations, and is acute in onset. Commonly reported symptoms are worsening breathlessness, cough, increased sputum production and change in sputum colour. The change in these symptoms often necessitates a change in medication.

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The diagnosis of an exacerbation is made clinically and does not depend on the results of investigations. However, investigations may sometimes be useful in ensuring appropriate treatment is given. Different investigation strategies are needed for people in hospital (who will tend to have more severe exacerbations) and people in the community.

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Both nebulisers and hand-held inhalers can be used to administer inhaled therapy during exacerbations of COPD. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The choice of delivery system should reflect the dose of drug needed, the person's ability to use the device, and the resources available to supervise therapy administration. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: When people are started on NIV there should be a clear plan covering what to do in the event of deterioration, and ceilings of therapy should be agreed. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Treat hospitalised exacerbations of COPD on intensive care units, including invasive ventilation when this is thought to be necessary. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider NIV for people who are slow to wean from invasive ventilation. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider physiotherapy using positive expiratory pressure devices for selected people with exacerbations of COPD, to help with clearing sputum. [2004, amended 2018]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Monitor people's recovery by regular clinical assessment of their symptoms and observation of their functional capacity. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Use pulse oximetry to monitor the recovery of people with non-hypercapnic, non-acidotic respiratory failure. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not routinely perform daily monitoring of peak expiratory flow (PEF) or FEV1 to monitor recovery from an exacerbation, because the magnitude of changes is small compared with the variability of the measurement. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Measure spirometry in all people before discharge. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Re-establish people on their optimal maintenance bronchodilator therapy before discharge. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: People who have had an episode of respiratory failure should have satisfactory oximetry or arterial blood gas results before discharge. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Assess all aspects of the routine care that people receive (including appropriateness and risk of side effects) before discharge. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Give people (or home carers) appropriate information to enable them to fully understand the correct use of medications, including oxygen, before discharge. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Make arrangements for follow-up and home care (such as visiting nurse, oxygen delivery or referral for other support) before discharge. [2004]

heading_path: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The person, their family and their physician should be confident that they can manage successfully before they are discharged. A formal activities of daily living assessment may be helpful when there is still doubt. [2004]
